evaluation_event.event: The user wants the AI assistant to help design a personalized weekly schedule balancing late-evening team meetings, CPAP protocol and wind-down routine, off-site client lunches, multi-generational caregiving responsibilities (especially evening family meals and medication times), and shared family calendar coordination—making sure his schedule is sustainable and respects his evolved sleep and family care requirements.
evaluation_event.user_prompt: I'm trying to set up a more sustainable weekly schedule with all my work and family commitments. Can you help me figure out how to work in off-site client lunches without disrupting my afternoon flow and family responsibilities?
evaluation_event.task_internal: 1. Integrate late-evening global calls and team meetings into the weekly calendar, avoiding conflicts with required wind-down/sleep preparation (do not prioritize early-morning slots). 2. Block out a strict two-hour pre-bed wind-down routine daily, reserve time for CPAP setup and sleep therapy compliance every night. 3. Exclude any dinner parties or group social events; limit evening gatherings to caregiving and immediate family. 4. Schedule short, off-site client lunches away from his desk and avoid double-booking with family commitments. 5. Ensure all planned activities—work, meals, care routines—are synced in the shared family calendar app. 6. Structure family meals and medication times around his father’s care needs, placing these as non-negotiable anchors in the evening routine.

required_preferences.fact: Adapts meeting schedule to accommodate late-evening global videoconferences and frequent time zone shifts, relinquishing exclusive early-morning scheduling in favor of firm-mandated hybrid hours and cross-continental team calls.

supersedes.fact: Schedules meetings in the early morning to maximize productivity before market open

required_preferences.fact: Enforces a strict two-hour wind-down routine before bed and uses a CPAP machine nightly, as directed for sleep apnea treatment, prioritizing sleep quality and structure over just quantity.

supersedes.fact: Prioritizes achieving a minimum of 8 hours of sleep nightly as part of his recovery plan

required_preferences.fact: No longer hosts dinner parties at home, instead keeps gatherings limited to immediate family and essential caregiving visitors due to live-in, multi-generational caregiving responsibilities.

supersedes.fact: Likes to host small dinner parties at home rather than attending large social events

required_preferences.fact: Rarely eats lunch at his desk—chooses short off-site client lunches for networking
Shares daily schedules in a shared family calendar app
Schedules family meals and evening routines around his father's care needs, including medication times and assisted dining, taking precedence over previous leisure or social activities.
